name: Stuffed Grape Leaves (Persian)
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Dried parsley, or to taste
1/4 teaspoon Black pepper, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a skillet, heat the olive oil and sauté the onion and mushrooms until soft.
2. Add parsley and spices. Stir in the peas and rice.
3. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
4. Line a 3-quart baking dish with a few grape leaves to keep the stuffed leaves from sticking and burning.
5. Place about one heaping tablespoon of the rice mixture (depending on the size of the leaf) in the center of a grape leaf.
6. Fold in the sides, then roll the leaf from the stem to the tip.
7. Repeat with the remaining grape leaves until all the rice mixture is used up.
8. Pour water into the bottom of the dish to prevent sticking and drying out.
9. Bake for 25 minutes. Serves 12 as an appetizer or 6 as an entrée.
Per serving (appetizer): 91 calories; 3g protein; 2.5g fat; 14g carbohydrates; 0 cholesterol; 94mg sodium.
